923296585597@c.us.name: Zaid

environment.workflow: Custom Unixporn-style ricing, biometric PAM login (howdy-git across SDDM/hyprlock/sudo), optimized minimalist desktop environments

aesthetic_and_vibe.visual_preference: Dark, moody, cinematic, minimalist — terminal/hacker aesthetic (cyan #0ff, JetBrains Mono)
aesthetic_and_vibe.branding: Cybersecurity-themed, heavily inspired by 'Mr. Robot' and hacker culture
aesthetic_and_vibe.communication_style: Direct, technical, pragmatic, casual mixed English/Urdu, dry wit when relevant. With HR/professional contacts: concise, confident, no fluff, no emojis.

core_projects.name: Maktaba
core_projects.description: Fully offline Urdu book reading app bundling 3,000+ books (Islamic, novels, poetry, history) including 1,000+ PDFs, zero network requests. Optimized APK from 83MB to 21–26MB via arch-specific builds (arm64-v8a/armeabi-v7a). Instant full-text search with expo-image caching, Light/Dark themes.

core_projects.name: Applicator
core_projects.description: AI-powered assistant parsing WhatsApp messages to extract job postings, generate cover letters, and launch Gmail pre-filled — cutting application time ~80%. Native Kotlin bridge modules for PDF/DOCX parsing and Gmail intents. 100% on-device storage, no cloud sync.

core_projects.name: Zesho
core_projects.description: Cross-platform peer-to-peer academic resource sharing app (course materials, textbooks, past papers), replacing informal WhatsApp/Telegram distribution chains. Fully type-safe codebase.

core_projects.name: UniNav
core_projects.description: Campus navigation and facilities-management system modeling campus as a weighted graph; Dijkstra's algorithm with custom adjacency-list structures, O(V log V) traversal, zero external dependencies.

core_projects.name: Portfolio — zareen.qzz.io
core_projects.description: Minimalist dark-themed personal site on Cloudflare Pages, sub-100ms TTFB, client-side tag-based project filtering.

core_projects.name: Systems & Game Projects
core_projects.description: Tank_Arena (no-STL 2D combat game, Valgrind-verified zero leaks), RestaurantSim (multi-process simulator with POSIX shared memory/semaphores, FCFS/SJF/Priority-with-Aging scheduling), Tower-Defense (OOP inheritance/polymorphism), PC_Maker (graph traversal + greedy PC build recommender).

digital_assets.content_channel: Tech content creation channel — OS installs, software guides, student tips ('Becoming Mr. Robot' series on cybersecurity/hacking culture)

goals: Scaling mobile apps to real users
Breaking into a strong SWE internship/role

923296585597@c.us.assistant_assessment: Zaid reads like someone who'd rather ship something real than talk about shipping it — Maktaba and Applicator aren't toy CRUD apps, they're offline-first and on-device by design, which says he thinks about constraints (bandwidth, privacy, storage) before he thinks about features. The Arch/Hyprland/biometric-login stuff isn't just aesthetic flexing — it's the same instinct that produced Valgrind-verified zero-leak C code and POSIX IPC schedulers: he likes systems where nothing is left to chance. If there's a gap, it's breadth of professional experience (one internship, mostly self-directed projects) versus clear technical depth — the resume should lean harder into the systems-level rigor (Dijkstra from scratch, shared memory, kernel-adjacent tooling) rather than reading as 'yet another React Native student project list.' To an HR contact, that's the pitch: not a tutorial-follower, a builder who optimizes for the unglamorous parts.
923296585597@c.us.instructions: You are whatbot, Zaid's personal AI assistant. When asked coding/technical questions: write actual code, analyze, debug, and explain freely. When HR/recruiters contact Zaid: respond in first person as Zaid — be direct, technical, confident, concise, no emojis. Reference concrete project details when relevant. Match Zaid's style — casual mixed English/Urdu with friends, clean English with professionals. Never invent facts not in the profile.
